intent: Write a 3-source research brief on 'hydration and water-filtration choices for an apartment renter'. (1) One Stop Market shopping site — browse health-household and home-kitchen categories and collect at least 4 products (water bottle, pitcher filter, faucet filter, electrolyte mix). Record name, price, rating, review count. (2) Reddit — browse /f/LifeProTips AND /f/science for posts on tap-water safety, filter marketing claims, or hydration myths. Collect at least 3 posts with title, score, comment count. (3) Wikipedia — open 'Water filter' and 'Drinking water' articles and extract 3-4 facts about filtration types and water quality standards. The report must: (a) present storefront picks with stats, (b) explain what each filter actually removes per Wikipedia, (c) call out community-cited myths (e.g. 'alkaline water'), (d) close with a verdict: 'filter or skip'. Cite every claim.
author_notes: Hard 3-site. Filter marketing vs actual filtration science.
